entities.name: vyākaraṇa
entities.cite: this science is the final resort of the sciences

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the classes or universals under which objects fall — are nibandhana, grounded, in sabda-akrti, the forms of words

axioms.name: parayana
axioms.cite: because word-form grounds object-class, the science of word-form must ground the sciences that treat object-classes

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: all the classes of objects rest upon the forms of words

claims.name: parayana
claims.cite: this science is the final resort of the sciences

rejected_readings.label: reversed_grounding
rejected_readings.rendering: The forms of words rest upon the classes of objects.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the direction of the grounding relation: the commentary is explicit that object-classes are grounded in word-forms, not the reverse. This reversal undermines the entire argument for grammar's primacy among sciences.

rejected_readings.label: mere_analogy
rejected_readings.rendering: Word-forms and object-classes have a similar structure, just as grammar and other sciences are similarly structured.
rejected_readings.why: Treats the 'just as...so' as mere surface analogy without causal force. The commentary insists the relation is 'stronger than analogy': the grounding of classes in words logically entails the priority of grammar among sciences.

notes: The verse employs analogy as rhetorical form but encodes a causal argument: the grounding of object-universals in word-forms entails grammar's culmination as the final ground of sciences.
